title: Water quality and health - Canada.ca
content:
## Water quality and health
Responsibility for drinking water safety in Canada, drinking water contaminants, recreational and drinking water guidelines, waste water, water reuse and technical resources.
- Per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS)
- Eat and drink safely abroad
- Drinking water treatment

## Service and information

### Drinking water and health
Who is responsible for keeping our drinking water supplies clean, safe and reliable, well water safety and drinking water contaminants.

### Well water and health
Build your well, test your water, protect and maintain your well, treat your well water, and well safety during and after emergencies.

### Recreational water and health
What recreational waters are, who is responsible for them, possible hazards, and how to protect yourself from exposure.

### Wastewater and health
Regulations, agreements, pollution, management and contacts.

### Household reclaimed or recycled water
Domestic water reuse and reclamation, safe use and guidelines.
Working with the provinces, territories, Indigenous communities, local authorities, scientists, and others.

### Water in First Nations communities
Working with First Nations to end long-term drinking water advisories and improve water and wastewater systems on reserve.

### Fluoride and oral health
Sources, benefits, health effects, proper use, and fluoride in drinking water.

### Consultation and Stakeholder Information Management System
Sign up to receive email notices about drinking water quality.
